A: Shall we play billiards?
B: Want to play a game of billiards?
C: Want to play pool?

standard: Dân lại khổ cực kỳ rồi này.
A: The people are suffering terribly again.
B: The people are going to suffer terribly again.
C: The people are suffering terribly again.

standard: hồi đó được như bây giờ thì ngon nhỉ?
A: Things would have been great back then, wouldn't they?
B: It would have been great if things back then were like they are now, wouldn't it?
C: If back then was like now, that would've been great, right?

standard: Sao phạt không bị lỗi mà đèn bị lỗi nhỉ?
A: Why is the traffic light faulty when there's no actual violation?
B: Why is the brake not malfunctioning, but the light is?
C: Why is it that [this thing] isn't broken but the lamp is broken?

standard: Làng nào mà nổ ghê vậy anh?
A: Which village had such a terrible explosion, sir?
B: Which village is bragging so much, brother?
C: Which village is so famous like that?

standard: Đúng cái vòng xoay này luôn này.
A: Right here, at this roundabout.
B: It's exactly this roundabout right here.
C: Right back to this same cycle again.

standard: Bữa nay mình ít lắm, trong Quảng Trị mưa nhiều nên có nhiều nấm.
A: We don't have many today; it's been raining a lot in Quang Tri, so there are many mushrooms.
B: I don't have many today; it has been raining a lot in Quang Tri, so there are many mushrooms.
C: Today I got very little. In Quảng Trị, it rained a lot so there are many mushrooms.

standard: nói vậy công an mời bây giờ.
A: If you say that, the police will invite you now.
B: Saying that will get you invited by the police now.
C: Then the police are calling you in now.

standard: lạnh lạnh làm nồi lẩu, ngẫm nghĩ lại sự đời thì vui biết mấy bạn mình nhỉ?
A: On a chilly day, enjoying a hot pot meal and reflecting on life would be so much fun, wouldn't it, my friend?
B: It's a bit chilly, so let's make a hotpot; reflecting on life would be so joyful, wouldn't it, my friends?
C: It's cold, so I'm making a pot of hot pot. Thinking about life, my friends and I are so happy, right?

standard: sao hay vậy Phương nhỉ, sao lại vứt quà bánh thế kia
A: That's strange, Phuong, why did you throw away the gifts and snacks like that?
B: How is that possible, Phuong? Why throw away the gifts and sweets like that?
C: How come, Phương? Why did you throw away the gifts like that?

standard: Quả này có thật ngoài đời không chị?
A: Does this fruit actually exist in real life?
B: Does this fruit actually exist in real life?
C: Is this thing for real in real life?

standard: Ông ở nhà làm nồi lẩu. Thích hơn nhỉ?
A: He'd rather stay home and make hot pot. Isn't that better?
B: I'll stay home and make a hotpot. That's better, right?
C: I'm home making a pot of hot pot. It's better this way, isn't it?

standard: Thảo nào em đi tìm từ nãy đến giờ.
A: No wonder I've been looking for you all this time.
B: No wonder I've been looking for it until now.
C: Thảo, why have I been looking for it since earlier?

standard: Quê hương thôi chị. Chứ nhà này ở đâu chứ không phải ở Hà Tĩnh.
A: It's my hometown, sister. This house isn't in Ha Tinh, though.
B: It's just my hometown, sister. As for this house, it's located somewhere else, not in Ha Tinh.
C: Just my hometown, sister. But this house is located somewhere, not in Hà Tĩnh.

standard: Đồ chua ăn với trứng vịt lộn cực ngon.
A: Pickled vegetables taste incredibly good when eaten with fertilized duck eggs.
B: Pickled vegetables eaten with fetal duck eggs are extremely delicious.
C: Sour pickled vegetables with balut are incredibly delicious.

standard: Đứa con nít thì chấp gì nó nhỉ?
A: Why bother arguing with a child?
B: Why hold it against a child?
C: Why would a child even care about that?

standard: Nghèo khổ thế này là do con cái sa đọa với mai thúy
A: This poverty is due to the children's depravity and drug addiction.
B: Being this poor is because the children are degenerate and use drugs.
C: We're so poor because the kids have gone astray with Mai Thúy.

standard: về già nhớ qua tìm tao như vậy nhé.
A: When you're old, remember to come find me like this, okay?
B: When we're old, remember to come find me like that.
C: When you get old, remember to come find me like that, okay.

standard: Không phải miền Tây. Mà bà ngoại là người miền Tây.
A: She's not from the Mekong Delta. But my grandmother is from the Mekong Delta.
B: It's not the Mekong Delta. But my maternal grandmother is from the Mekong Delta.
C: Not the Western region. It's just that my grandmother is from the Western region.

standard: Mới chỉ báo hè này con có thể không về quê thôi mà ba mẹ văn cỡ đó
A: I only mentioned that I might not be coming home this summer, and my parents are already making such a fuss.
B: I only just mentioned that I might not be able to go back to my hometown this summer, yet my parents' reaction is that dramatic.
C: I just told them I might not come back home this summer, but my parents are not going to accept that.

standard: lớn rồi có con rồi không được hỗn nhé
A: You're grown up now, you have children, so don't be disrespectful.
B: You're grown up and have children now, so don't be disrespectful.
C: You're all grown up and have kids now, don't be naughty anymore, okay?

standard: Muốn làm rể Kiên Giang để thử tửu lượng một phát xem nào
A: I want to become a son-in-law in Kien Giang to test my drinking capacity.
B: I want to become a son-in-law of Kien Giang to test my alcohol tolerance once.
C: Want to become a son-in-law in Kiên Giang just to test my alcohol tolerance once and see

standard: Trong lành phải biết luôn, bữa nào ta dạo một bữa nhé?
A: The air is so fresh, let's go for a walk sometime, shall we?
B: It's so fresh, I have to say; shall we take a stroll there sometime?
C: Trong lành should know this by now too, when can we hang out together?

standard: tao xấu hổ quá bây ơi!
A: I'm so embarrassed, man!
B: I'm so embarrassed, guys!
C: I'm so embarrassed!

standard: Chắc cũng chưa anh ạ. Em chưa thấy đeo túi đó.
A: Probably not, sir. I haven't seen him carrying that bag.
B: Probably not yet. I haven't seen them wearing that bag.
C: Probably not yet, anh. I haven't seen anyone wear that bag.

standard: Năm nay lấy chồng được chưa?
A: Is it okay to get married this year?
B: Can I get married this year?
C: Have you gotten married this year yet?

standard: lễ hội quế không biết như nào các chị nhỉ, xem họ phơi quế à?
A: I wonder what the cinnamon festival is like, ladies? Do you see them drying cinnamon?
B: I don't know what the cinnamon festival is like, you guys; do we just watch them dry the cinnamon?
C: I wonder what the cinnamon festival is like, girls. Is it just watching them dry cinnamon?

standard: sao em mình bi quan thế nhỉ?
A: Why is my younger sibling so pessimistic?
B: Why is my younger sibling so pessimistic?
C: Why am I so pessimistic?

standard: em mà thấy sợi tóc bạc nào lòi ra là ngày đó không muốn ăn cơm.
A: If I see even a single gray hair sticking out, I won't want to eat that day.
B: If I see a single grey hair sticking out, I won't want to eat that day.
C: If I see any gray hair sticking out, that day I don't feel like eating rice.

standard: Lo mà sửa đi, chứ bữa này tôi đi qua bất ngờ rồi.
A: Get it fixed, because I was surprised when I passed by today.
B: You'd better fix it, because I might drop by unexpectedly these days.
C: Go ahead and fix it, because I already passed by unexpectedly this time.

standard: Tui ở tận ở ngoài đấy mà bên trong vẫn nghe luôn hả
A: I was all the way out there, and they could still hear everything inside?
B: I'm all the way out here, but can you still hear me from inside?
C: I'm way out there outside but still hear it from inside?

standard: Càng màu mè càng dễ toi.
A: The more flashy the colors, the easier it is to fail.
B: The more flashy it is, the easier it is to fail.
C: The more you show off, the quicker you fall.

standard: Thật không trời, để học kẻo anh ấy kiểm tra bài.
A: Oh no, I need to study before he gives me a test.
B: Seriously? I'd better study in case he checks my work.
C: Seriously, let me study in case he checks our homework.

standard: Lấy rồi bỏ mà lấy mần chi
A: What's the point of taking something and then discarding it?
B: Why take it just to throw it away?
C: What was the point of taking it if you're just going to abandon it anyway?

standard: Tôi được cái hô hào thôi chứ cha làm là chính.
A: I'm only good at shouting slogans, but my father is the one who does most of the work.
B: I'm only good at cheering others on, while my dad is the one who actually does the work.
C: I just do the calling out, but you're the one who actually does the work.

standard: để sau này em nhường anh nhé chứ người ta chửi em quá trời
A: I'll give way to you later, because people are cursing me so much.
B: I'll let you have it later, because people are scolding me so much.
C: Fine, I'll let you win next time, since people criticize me way too much

standard: Ngồi đợi mãi mà có thấy đội nào đi đến đâu. Tài thật.
A: I waited and waited, but no team came. How strange.
B: I've been sitting and waiting forever, but I don't see any team arriving. Unbelievable.
C: Been sitting here waiting forever but haven't seen any team get anywhere. Impressive.

standard: Thôi chị đừng mơ nữa. Vừa rồi chị mơ là nhà em ngập đấy. Em liệt đi tê.
A: Stop dreaming, sister. You just dreamt that my house was flooded. I was paralyzed.
B: Stop dreaming. You just dreamed that my house was flooded. I'm totally paralyzed.
C: Stop dreaming, don't expect that anymore. You were just wishing my house would get flooded. Are you out of your mind?

standard: Tối nay tôi bận làm rồi ông
A: I'm busy working tonight, sir.
B: I'm busy working tonight, man.
C: I'm busy working tonight.

standard: Đường về bà ngoại lại gần thêm chút nữa rồi.
A: The road to Grandma's is getting a little closer now.
B: The road back to my grandmother's house has become a bit shorter.
C: The road to my grandmother's house is getting even closer now.

standard: Nghĩ lấy nhau đến nơi rồi mà.
A: We thought we were about to get married.
B: I thought we were finally going to be together.
C: It's gotten to the point where we're thinking about getting married.

standard: Mong là đừng bị gì, chứ lo.
A: Hopefully nothing bad will happen, otherwise I'm worried.
B: I hope nothing happens, because I'm worried.
C: Hope nothing happens, but I'm worried.

standard: Sáng qua tôi mới chọc mấy chị môi trường. Vật xuống làm gì để giờ phải chổng mông đở lên.
A: Yesterday morning I teased the environmental ladies. Why did you throw it down only to have to lift it up with your butt sticking up in the air?
B: Yesterday morning, I just teased the sanitation workers. Why did I mess with them, and now I have to scramble to make things right.
C: Yesterday morning I just poked at some bees, and now I have to stick my butt up to catch them as they fall.

standard: Lão đó nghe nói khét tiếng chuyên gia lừa gái đó bạn à
A: That old man is notorious for being a womanizer, you know.
B: I heard that guy is notorious for being an expert at scamming girls.
C: I heard that guy is notorious as a womanizer, you know.

standard: tới công chuyện thật ấy chứ
A: Let's get down to business.
B: This is really getting serious.
C: Let's get to the real business then

standard: Chỉ sợ bữa nay cầu hôn mai phải đi cầu hồn thôi.
A: I'm afraid that if you propose today, you'll have to propose tomorrow.
B: I'm just afraid that if I propose today, I'll have to perform a séance tomorrow.
C: I'm just afraid that if I propose today, tomorrow I'll be attending a funeral.

standard: sau này đi xe khách đừng có nói vậy nhé, ông ấy đánh mày tao can không kịp
A: Next time you're on a bus, don't say that, okay? If he hits you, I won't be able to stop him.
B: In the future, when taking a passenger bus, don't say things like that; if he hits you, I won't be able to stop him in time.
C: From now on, when taking the bus, don't say things like that - he almost hit you and I couldn't stop him in time.

standard: chủ xe này gạt mưa kêu như máy xay gạo nhỉ?
A: This car's windshield wipers sound like a rice mill, don't they?
B: This car owner's windshield wipers sound like a rice grinder, don't they?
C: My windshield wiper is making noise like a rice grinder, huh?

standard: Đang tính sáng nay vào Vinh nữa chứ.
A: I was planning to go to Vinh this morning too.
B: I was actually planning to go into Vinh again this morning.
C: I was thinking about going to Vinh this morning, you know.

standard: Gì mà khổ vậy nhỉ?
A: Why is it so difficult?
B: Why is it so miserable?
C: Why is it this hard?

standard: Tầm này có miếng thịt nghé mà hấp nhỉ?
A: How about some steamed veal at this time of year?
B: At this time, it would be great to have some steamed veal, wouldn't it?
C: At this time, I wish I had some steamed veal.

standard: làm tô bún giò, tưới thêm nước mắm ớt vẫn là chân ái
A: Making a bowl of pork knuckle noodle soup and adding some chili fish sauce is still the best.
B: Making a bowl of pork knuckle noodles and drizzling some chili fish sauce over it is still the true love.
C: Made a bowl of bún giò, drizzled with more chili fish sauce, still the ultimate love.

standard: ông ấy ngại vợ ông ấy nên ông ấy nói vậy đấy.
A: He said that because he was embarrassed in front of his wife.
B: He is afraid of his wife, so that's why he said that.
C: He's afraid of his wife, so he said that.

standard: Rồi ngày nay xe nào gửi đây
A: Which car is parked here today?
B: So which car is being sent here today?
C: So nowadays, which car can even be parked here?

standard: hôm nào dắt tôi về Kiên Giang chuyến nào
A: When are you going to take me to Kien Giang for a trip?
B: Take me to Kien Giang sometime.
C: Take me to Kiên Giang sometime, any trip will do.

standard: 8 củ 1 chỉ cơ mà.
A: It's 8 taels and 1 mace, though.
B: It's 8 million per mace, though.
C: 8 million dong but 1 chỉ though.

standard: về Cần Thơ miền sông nước chơi một bữa này
A: Let's go to Can Tho, the land of rivers and waterways, for a meal.
B: Go to Can Tho, the land of rivers and canals, for a visit this time.
C: Heading to Can Tho, the land of rivers, let's hang out one of these times

standard: Tiền bỏ đâu cho hết kìa bà này.
A: Where did all that money go, woman?
B: Where on earth is this woman going to put all that money.
C: Where did all the money go, woman?

standard: Những món quà thiết thực. Hoa hòe làm gì tội cho mấy người thu gom rác.
A: Practical gifts are better. Why bother with flowers and stuff, it's a burden for the garbage collectors.
B: Practical gifts. Why bother with flowers; it's just a burden for the waste collectors.
C: Practical gifts. What sin did the [discarded items] commit to be blamed on garbage collectors.

standard: Dân văn phòng mà ăn quán này 80k một suất thì các bạn Quảng Ninh giàu quá.
A: If office workers can afford to pay 80,000 VND per meal at this restaurant, then the people of Quang Ninh must be incredibly rich.
B: If office workers eat at this place for 80k per serving, then you people in Quang Ninh are too rich.
C: As office workers, if you eat at this restaurant where a meal costs 80k, then you guys in Quảng Ninh must be so rich.

standard: Dân nào rồi cũng người này người khác, đừng vơ đũa cả nắm
A: People of all kinds are different; don't generalize.
B: No matter the people, there are always different individuals; don't paint everyone with the same brush.
C: People are all different, so don't lump them all together.

standard: Tôi cũng là con người mà ít nhiều gì tôi cũng phải biết chứ.
A: I'm a human being too, so I should know something, at least to some extent.
B: I am also a human being, so one way or another, I should know.
C: I'm also human, so I should at least know some things.

standard: Vinh dạo này không đùa được đâu nhỉ?
A: Vinh is no joke these days, is he?
B: Vinh isn't someone to be messed with lately, is he?
C: I can't joke around these days, huh?

standard: Chợ Nghèn! Nhiều thứ tươi ngon lắm! Gì cũng muốn mua!
A: Nghèn Market! So many fresh and delicious things! I want to buy everything!
B: Nghèn Market! There are so many fresh and delicious things! I want to buy everything!
C: Nghèn Market! So many fresh and delicious things! I want to buy everything!

standard: Người ta bắt được cá mà sao vợ chồng chị không bắt được vậy nhỉ.
A: Other people can catch fish, so why can't you and your husband catch any?
B: Other people are catching fish, so why aren't you and your husband catching any?
C: How come others could catch fish but my husband and I couldn't?

standard: Ngon, đã luôn nhỉ, sắp xếp mời bữa nữa.
A: Delicious, always great! Let's arrange another meal together.
B: Delicious, so satisfying, let's arrange another meal soon.
C: It's always great, I should arrange to treat everyone to another meal.

standard: về nói ngoại đi đùn cốm cho mà ăn.
A: Tell Grandma to make rice flakes for you to eat.
B: Go home and tell grandma to give me some green rice flakes to eat.
C: Go tell grandmother to make some cốm for you to eat.

standard: vậy chưa thấy pháo Lao Bảo với Tân Thành rồi?
A: So, you haven't seen the artillery at Lao Bao and Tan Thanh yet?
B: So, I haven't seen the Lao Bao and Tan Thanh artillery yet?
C: So you haven't seen the fireworks at Lao Bao and Tan Thanh yet?

standard: hôm nào về chở con gái qua đây chơi.
A: When you come home, bring your daughter here to play.
B: Some day when you return, bring your daughter over here to play.
C: Someday, come back and bring your daughter over here to visit.

standard: Con gái nhà ai mà xinh gái thế này nhỉ!
A: Whose daughter is this? She's so beautiful!
B: Whose daughter is this, she's so beautiful!
C: Whose daughter is this? She's so pretty!

standard: Nghèo cái gì, bữa nay có pháo quốc phòng thì tiệc gì cũng xách ra đốt.
A: What's there to be poor about? Today we have national defense artillery, so we'll bring it out and set it off at any party.
B: Poor? No way, now that I have defense fireworks, I'll bring them out to set off at any party.
C: What poverty? If we have defense fireworks today, any party, we'll just grab them and set them off.

standard: Vậy người yêu cũ không có trong này à?
A: So, your ex isn't in here?
B: So the ex isn't in here?
C: So the ex isn't among these?

standard: con có học trường này đâu?
A: My child doesn't go to this school.
B: I don't study at this school.
C: I don't even go to this school.

standard: muốn đi dữ ấy chúng mày ơi
A: I really want to go, guys!
B: I really want to go, you guys.
C: I want to go there, you guys

standard: không ăn thì cho em chứ sao vứt đi vậy?
A: If you're not going to eat it, give it to me instead of throwing it away!
B: If you're not eating it, give it to me instead of throwing it away.
C: If you're not going to eat it, just give it to me. Why would you throw it away like that?

standard: Cái bảng chỉ đường nó to khủng khiếp!
A: That signpost is absolutely enormous!
B: The signpost is terrifyingly huge!
C: The directional sign is huge!

standard: Nước vui là uống vào nó tươi đấy, còn không uống là mặt y như vậy đấy.
A: The water is refreshing when you drink it, but if you don't drink it, your face will look just like that.
B: Happy water makes you look fresh when you drink it; otherwise, your face looks just like that.
C: The refreshing drink is that when you drink it, it's refreshing, but if you don't drink it, your face stays exactly the same.

standard: chờ khi nào rụng răng mới có bạn tôi này?
A: I'll only have a friend like you when my teeth fall out?
B: When will my teeth fall out before I finally have a friend?
C: How long do I have to wait until my teeth fall out just to get this friend of mine?

standard: tao là thầy bói này, tao nói mà mày không tin!
A: I'm a fortune teller, and you don't believe me!
B: I'm a fortune teller, I'm telling you but you don't believe me!
C: I'm a fortune teller, I'm telling you but you don't believe me!

standard: Cũng gần nhà tôi
A: It's also near my house.
B: It's also near my house.
C: It's also near my house

standard: Hôm đó ăn no căng ruột luôn là biết rồi, có chừng nào ăn chừng nấy
A: That day I ate until I was completely full, you know what I meant; I ate as much as I could get.
B: I was stuffed to the brim that day, as you know; I ate as much as I could.
C: That day I ate until I was completely stuffed, obviously already knowing it, whatever amount there was I ate that much

standard: rét kệ nó chứ sao lo ác vậy.
A: Just ignore the cold weather, why worry so much?
B: Let it be cold, why are you so worried?
C: Just leave them alone, why are you so worried?

standard: Dân Hà Huy Tập thì gia nhập gì vậy bạn.
A: What kind of association do the people from Ha Huy Tap join?
B: What should people from Ha Huy Tap join?
C: What do the people on Hà Huy Tập usually join?

standard: bảo vô trong đó quậy chứ đừng quậy ở quê
A: Tell them to cause trouble in the countryside, not in the village.
B: I told them to go in there and cause trouble, just don't cause trouble in the countryside.
C: go ahead and be rowdy inside, but don't be rowdy in the countryside

standard: Ngô ngày xưa ngon, bây giờ có chỗ trộn thêm cơm nguội ăn nó sai quá
A: Corn used to be delicious, but now some places mix it with leftover rice, and it tastes so wrong.
B: Corn used to be delicious, but now there are places that mix in leftover rice, which tastes completely wrong.
C: Corn used to be so delicious, but now there are places that mix in leftover rice—it's really wrong to eat that.

standard: để tôi hôm nào về lái thử chiếc đấy.
A: I'll test drive that car sometime when I get back.
B: Let me try driving that one some day when I go back.
C: Let me go test drive that one some day.

standard: Lên mỗi 4 kg thôi, chẳng lẽ núp mình đến tết rồi ra gặp mọi người
A: I've only gained 4 kg, should I just hide until Tet (Lunar New Year) and then come out to meet everyone?
B: I only gained 4 kg, but should I just stay in hiding until Tet and then go see everyone?
C: I've only gained 4 kg, would I really have to hide until Tet and then come out to see everyone?

standard: không có mà , nhưng làm ơn nói nhỏ nhỏ được không, nói to thế lộ hết mất
A: No, there isn't, but please speak softly, you'll reveal everything if you speak so loudly.
